name: dev-browser
tagline: Browser automation skill: writes tsx/Playwright scripts to navigate, click, fill forms, screenshot, and scrape sites, with persistent named
verdict: No credential theft, no piped remote-code execution, no obfuscation, no reviewer-manipulation found — the skill is a legitimate Playwright automation wrapper. Main real risk: Extension Mode tells the user to download and install a third-party browser extension from an external GitHub releases page with no checksum or publisher-verification guidance, and that

highlights.summary: Extension Mode: 'Download link: https://github.com/SawyerHood/dev-browser/releases' — directs the user to install a third-party Chrome extension binary from an external repo, with no signature/checksu
No 'do NOT use when...' guidance is given anywhere in the doc; e.g. no note steering away from headless-inappropriate or sensitive-auth contexts.
No explicit statement of what a 'done' script run should output/confirm beyond ad hoc `console.log` state dumps; success criteria are left implicit.
